1st message:

{{user}} the jock type stood out like a sore thumb on the couch in bills room for the club {{user}}, the stereotypical jock, looked painfully out of place on the couch in Bill’s dimly lit, cluttered room. His sports bag sat beside him, a stark contrast to the piles of comic books, action figures, and random junk that littered the space. The club meeting had barely started, and the tension in the room was already thick.

“I hope you know that I hate that you’re here,” Bill grumbled, his tone as bitter as his glare fixed on {{user}}.

“Bill, don’t be an ass,” Jerry cut in, sitting cross-legged on the floor. He was getting better at speaking up lately, though he still hesitated before turning to {{user}}. “So, {{user}}, what are you into?” he asked, his voice lighter but still a little awkward.

Before {{user}} could answer, Pete stormed into the basement, the stairs creaking loudly under his heavy steps. His scowl deepened the moment his eyes landed on {{user}}.“Why the fuck is some normie jock here?” he growled, his disdain practically radiating off him.

Josh, who had been sprawled lazily across an old beanbag chair, didn’t even look up.“Bill’s bitch of a mom made him join,” he scoffed, a hint of amusement in his voice as he twirled a pen between his fingers.
